key_insight: TANS-FO closes the gap between clinical text prescriptions and orthosis geometry by combining text-to-lattice design with real-time biomechanical surrogate feedback.
engineering_relevance: The prototype enables rapid, manufacturing-ready customized foot orthosis generation with surrogate-based plantar-stress validation instead of slow iterative FEM loops.
method: Text-aligned neural surrogate maps clinical intent to a continuous lattice-density field via cross-attention.
Graph neural network surrogate predicts plantar stress in real time as a substitute for Abaqus-based FEA.
Closed-loop pipeline iteratively aligns design output with biomechanical response on the PicoFoot-5K cohort.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports GNN surrogate agreement with Abaqus at R^2 = 0.94, a 34.7% surrogate-predicted peak-pressure reduction over parametric CAD, and a 0.42 mm fit error, with a small exploratory VAS comfort study (n = 12) marked as preliminary only.
watch_item: This is a research prototype, not a clinically validated therapy, and the observational pain data lacks a control group and short-term follow-up limits claims of efficacy.
paper_title: A Research Prototype for Closed-Loop Generative Design of Customized Foot Orthoses via Semantic-Physics Alignment
